items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm tired of my playlist. It's all the same pop singers I've been streaming for years, and everything sounds identical now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You should try some indie folk, then. There's this artist, well, she mixes acoustic guitar with these quiet electronic loops. Totally different from the radio stuff.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, I've never really given folk a chance. Isn't it kind of slow?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Some of it, sure, but her newer album is actually upbeat. And she's playing a small show downtown next month.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, seriously? Maybe I'll listen to the album first and see if I like her before I commit to tickets.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main concern at the start of the conversation?

options.A: She is bored with the music she usually listens to
options.B: She cannot afford concert tickets downtown
options.C: She dislikes every kind of folk music
options.D: She lost access to her streaming playlist

questions.explanation: 开头女生说自己厌倦了播放列表，都是同样的流行歌手，听起来千篇一律，所以 A 正确。B 只是后面顺带提到的演唱会，不是她一开始的核心烦恼，属于以偏概全。C 与事实相反，她只是没接触过民谣、还问是不是很慢，并未讨厌所有民谣。D 完全是另一个话题，她并没有失去播放列表的访问权限。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.C: Listen to the artist's album before deciding on tickets
options.D: Ask the man to build her a new playlist

questions.explanation: 女生明确说会先听专辑，看自己是否喜欢，再决定买不买票，所以 C 正确。A 是她明确拒绝的做法——她说要先听再决定，不会马上买票。B 属于过度推断，对话里从未提到要删掉旧歌单。D 与对话无关，她没有请男生帮她做歌单。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you end up seeing that new mystery movie last night?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I caught the late show. The plot twists were incredible, I honestly didn't see the ending coming at all.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, nice! So you'd recommend it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Mostly. My only complaint is that it dragged on. It was almost three hours, and the middle part felt really slow.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Three hours? Wow, I usually can't sit still that long.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then maybe wait for the streaming release. That way you can pause and take a break whenever you need to.

questions.stem: What did the man dislike about the movie?

options.A: The plot twists were easy to predict

questions.explanation: 男生说唯一的抱怨是电影太拖沓，将近三小时，中段很慢，所以 B 正确。A 与事实相反，他称赞反转很精彩、没猜到结局。C 也错，他说没料到结局，是褒义，不是失望。D 从未提及，属于合理但未出现的信息。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: See the movie in theaters tonight
options.B: Decide never to watch the movie
options.C: Ask the man to watch it again with her
options.D: Wait to watch the movie once it streams

questions.explanation: 男生建议她等流媒体上线，这样能随时暂停休息，而女生说自己坐不住三小时，因此她很可能等流媒体，D 正确。A 与男生的建议相悖，属于被否定的选项。B 属于过度推断，她只是坐不住那么久，并没有说完全不看。C 与对话无关，没人提到再看一遍。

items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I really want to get more involved on campus this semester, but I can't decide between the photography club or the intramural volleyball team.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, how much time can you actually spare? The volleyball team practices four evenings a week, so that's a big commitment.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Four evenings? Oh, that's a lot. I've got labs running late most nights already.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. The photography club only meets on Saturdays, and you can skip a week if you're busy. It's way more flexible.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds much more realistic for my schedule. I think I'll sign up for the club.

questions.stem: How often does the volleyball team practice?
questions.explanation: 男生明确说排球队一周练四个晚上，所以 C 正确。A 其实是摄影俱乐部的安排（周六），被错误地安到排球队头上。B 把数字改错了。D 也是错误细节，练习在晚上，不是只在周末。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.B: Try out for the volleyball team
options.D: Wait until next semester to decide

questions.explanation: 女生说俱乐部更符合她的时间安排，并说打算报名俱乐部，所以 A 正确。B 是她放弃的选项，因为排球队占用太多晚上。C 属于过度推断，她只是说晚上要做实验，从没说要退掉实验。D 与对话相悖，她已经当场做了决定，不会拖到下学期。

items.situation: two friends sharing cooking tips while grocery shopping

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I always buy the same three vegetables. I want to cook something new this week, but I never know where to start.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You know what helped me? Roasting. You just toss almost any vegetable with olive oil and salt, then bake it. It's really hard to mess up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? That sounds way easier than the complicated recipes I keep failing at.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. Start with something firm like sweet potatoes or carrots. Grab a few, and I'll text you the timing later.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That'd be great. Okay, let me throw some sweet potatoes in the cart.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Which vegetables are the healthiest to eat
options.B: How to grow vegetables at home
options.C: How to follow a complicated recipe
options.D: An easy way to cook new vegetables

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女生想做点新菜、男生教她用烤的方法轻松烹饪蔬菜，所以 D 正确。A 是另一个话题，两人没有比较蔬菜营养。B 也无关，讨论的是烹饪不是种菜。C 与情境相反，女生正是想避开复杂食谱，改用简单方法。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Sign up for a cooking class
options.B: Buy sweet potatoes to roast at home
options.C: Keep buying only her usual three vegetables
options.D: Ask the man to cook dinner for her

questions.explanation: 女生最后说要把红薯放进购物车，而男生刚推荐用烤红薯入门，所以她很可能买红薯回去烤，B 正确。A 与对话无关，没人提到烹饪课。C 与她的意图相反，她正是想尝试新东西、不再买老三样。D 属于过度推断，她只是请男生发时间给她，并没有让男生代做晚饭。

items.situation: two students figuring out a time to meet for a group project

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We need to find a time for our whole group to work on the history presentation. Are you free tomorrow afternoon?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Tomorrow afternoon I've got my part-time shift at the café until five. Could we do the morning instead?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Morning works for me. How about ten o'clock in the library?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Ten is perfect. Should I message the other two to make sure they can make it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, please do. If they can't, we'll just have to find another slot.

questions.stem: When do the speakers agree to meet?
questions.explanation: 男生下午要打工到五点，提议改早上，女生定在十点图书馆，男生说完美，所以 A 正确。B 把五点这个下班时间当成了见面时间，是错误细节。C 张冠李戴，咖啡馆是男生打工的地方，不是见面地点。D 也错，约的是上午不是晚上。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.B: Reschedule the meeting for the afternoon
options.C: Contact the other two group members
options.D: Start the presentation on his own

questions.explanation: 男生主动问要不要通知另外两个人，女生说好，所以他接下来很可能去联系其他两名组员，C 正确。A 属于过度推断，他只是改约到早上，没说要取消打工。B 与结论相反，他们已经把时间从下午改成了早上。D 与对话无关，没人提到他要独自开始做展示。
